assertions.expectation: Checks for deprecated API usage that may have changed behavior in 1.30
assertions.reason: The response mentions deprecated API usage for 1.32, not 1.30 as specified in the expectation

assertions.expectation: Recommends checking admission webhooks — they may be rejecting or slowing down requests on the new version
assertions.reason: Section 4 explicitly covers admission webhook compatibility with specific commands to check webhook configurations and failures

assertions.expectation: Advises checking if resource requests/limits need adjusting for any system components that changed
assertions.reason: Section 3 includes commands to check system component resource usage and mentions that components like coredns, metrics-server may need more resources

assertions.expectation: Suggests examining kube-system pods for any that are crashlooping or restarting
assertions.reason: Section 3 includes commands to check kube-system pod health and events, which would reveal crashlooping or restarting pods

assertions.expectation: Includes commands to check API server metrics and latency (kubectl get --raw /metrics)
assertions.reason: Section 1 includes the command 'kubectl get --raw /metrics | grep apiserver_request_total | grep deprecated' to check API server metrics

assertions.expectation: Recommends checking if NetworkPolicy or service mesh components need updating for the new version
assertions.reason: The response does not specifically mention NetworkPolicy or service mesh components needing updates

assertions.expectation: Advises reviewing pod events for scheduling delays or readiness probe failures
assertions.reason: Multiple sections include commands to check events, including kubectl get events commands that would show scheduling and readiness probe issues

assertions.expectation: Mentions checking if HPA or VPA are behaving differently on the new version
assertions.reason: Section 2 specifically covers HPA/VPA behavioral changes with commands to check HPA status and scaling behavior changes
